hard.next_step: 6.29.26 KK - Alexa to share the updates with ANA that we put together for McAfee RE: SMS/whatsapp/etc. to showcase our consistent innovation vs SAP. Hoping this action generates a response one way or another on the outcome of the SAP beta
hard.ais_why: Buyer-driven AI reference demand at the 21 Jan 2026 onsite, active ANA POC track (Feb-Apr 2026), $1.5M SAP Ariba replacement with Merlin in scope.

forecast_read.reason: Upside Key Deal with 22 days to close is not defensible: the buyer has not replied since 22 May 2026, the SAP Ariba beta outcome is unresolved, and the POC, AI references, updated commercials and all contracting work remain unstarted. This is a date-and-forecast problem layered on a genuinely at-risk competitive position.
forecast_read.math: Created 6 Mar 2024 (28 months in cycle); Shortlisted since the Jan 2026 onsite (~6 months in stage vs an ~18-day expected buyer cadence). Close date pushed three times (18 Mar: 31 Mar to 31 May; 20 May: to 30 Jun; 16 Jun: to 31 Jul), a net 4-month forward slip. Last two-way buyer engagement 22 May 2026, 48 days before today (9 Jul 2026). Remaining gates (beta outcome, POC, 2 reference calls, updated commercials, contracting) cannot complete in 22 days; realistic close is Q4 2026 at the earliest.

metrics.narrative: Pain is well documented but never quantified in dollars by the buyer. David Duchon (demo#3, 26 Jun 2025) flagged that automated invoicing 'faced challenges due to a lack of connection to contracts or purchase orders,' and demo#2 (25 Jun 2025) surfaced a spend-visibility gap where a $1,000,000 janitorial contract entry sat against actual spend closer to $5,000,000. No ROI or savings figure has been stated by Allstate at any point in the record; quantifying the case remains an open move and matters more now that the decision sits with leadership (Gaurav, 22 May 2026).

economic_buyer.narrative: Casey McDowell (VP - Global Enterprise Procurement, Executive Sponsor contact role) is the mapped economic buyer: Gaurav offered to broker a Chicago session with him after the 21 Jan 2026 onsite ('gaurav supporting us on setting up session w casey in chicago') and the rep pursued it through February. But Casey has had no evidenced direct engagement since, and on 22 May 2026 Gaurav said 'Let me connect with our leadership and take their inputs on how to proceed' with no reply in 48 days, so the actual approving leadership is engaged only indirectly and the outcome of that consult is unknown. Cheryl Harris (SVP Procurement) and Jess Merten (President, Property-Liability) hold sponsor/evaluator roles but show no engagement in the record.

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer named its live criteria at the 21 Jan 2026 onsite: realistic SAP lift-and-shift implementation experience ('Give us some realistic picture wherever you have done the lift and shift... what challenges you face... what is the timeline'), proof that Zycus AI is used in production at reference customers ('that confidence in terms of customer reference... in terms of your AI journey, that was little uncertain'), and the forward roadmap given their ~1.5-year runway. The formal RFP round is behind them and went well by the buyer's own account ('fabulous job from an RFP presentations point of view', Nags-side speaker, 21 Jan 2026). What is not visible is how these criteria are being scored against the parallel SAP Ariba beta.
sources: Avoma transcript, Allstate and Zycus Product Session, 2026-01-21

decision_process.narrative: The process was explicit in January: a formal SAP-vs-Zycus bake-off confirmed 15 Dec 2025, a decision expected 'in next 3/4 months' from the 21 Jan 2026 onsite, and the SAP Ariba contract expiring roughly 12 months out as the forcing function. That window has now passed unresolved: since 21 May 2026 the buyer has been running an internal SAP Ariba workshop/beta program, and on 22 May Gaurav routed the go-forward decision to unnamed leadership. The current process step, who decides and by when, is genuinely unknown, which is the single biggest read gap on the deal.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics exist anywhere in the record: no MSA, SOW, procurement or legal contact engagement, and no discussion of Allstate's paper path despite a 31 Jul 2026 close date. Legal contacts exist in the contact roles (Elliot Stultz, Travis Rogers, Bethanie Berube) but none show any activity. The only forcing function on paper is the SAP Ariba contract expiry, roughly 12 months from Jan 2026 ('resigning ariba... expires in 12 months', Next Step 2026-01-21). This is a genuine knowledge gap, consistent with a deal that has not yet been decided.

identify_pain.narrative: Concrete, confirmed pain across the Jun-Jul 2025 demo series: manual CLM redlining requiring legal involvement and disparate homegrown systems for supplier risk and contract renewals (demo#2, 25 Jun 2025), invoices failing to tie back to POs/contracts despite functioning OCR (demo#3, 26 Jun 2025, David Duchon), and Fieldglass/SharePoint fragmentation for contingent-labor timesheets with a stated desire for 'a single touch point... for invoice reconciliation as well as time management' (VMS demo, 15 Jul 2025). Pain ownership spans Gaurav's S2P operations team and AP. The pain is real and old; what has never been established is its dollar cost.

champion.narrative: Gaurav (Rakesh Desai), the sourcing lead for the S2P RFP (self-described on the 21 Jan 2026 onsite), is the only consistent day-to-day contact and has done coach-like work: coordinating the onsite, brokering the Casey session, and engaging on POC logistics through May. But he behaves as a process gatekeeper, not an advocate: he runs the parallel SAP Ariba beta without objection, his cadence is reactive (PTO-driven reschedules in Mar 2026), and he has been silent since 22 May 2026 despite three follow-ups (8 Jun, 20 May and 27 Apr threads). No stronger internal champion has emerged in 28 months. This is a real gap at a decision moment.

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the incumbent and the single active threat: Allstate is running a dedicated 'SAP Ariba workshop/beta program' in parallel (Gaurav, 21 May 2026) and that beta is directly consuming the technology resources needed to schedule the Zycus POC. The buyer stated on 21 Jan 2026 that it is not leaning either way ('Are you leaning one way or the other now? No... We are not'). Coupa and Ivalua sit in the Salesforce competitor field with no corroborating call, email, or next-step evidence and read as dormant. SAP Fieldglass is a scoped VMS incumbent Zycus explicitly integrates with rather than displaces (VMS demo, 15 Jul 2025); Beeline and Icertis were single passing mentions in 2025 and are dormant. The Zycus wedge is agentic AI in production (ANA, Merlin Intake) versus SAP's still-beta story, but that wedge remains unproven to this buyer because the POC and AI references never happened.

critical_signals.text: Allstate has been running an internal SAP Ariba workshop and beta since at least 21 May 2026, and by the buyer's own statement that program is consuming the very technology resources needed to run the Zycus POC.

The buyer's last substantive word was 22 May 2026, when Gaurav said he would take the go-forward question to leadership and come back; 48 days and three follow-ups later there has been no reply, and the decision window the buyer set in January (three to four months) has passed.

The updated proposal Allstate has been expecting since mid-April 2026 has never been discussed, and the close date has been pushed three times since March, now sitting 22 days out with no commercial dialogue live.
At the 21 Jan 2026 onsite the buyer made AI-in-production customer references a decision condition, and the two AI-adoption references Zycus lined up in response have still not been delivered.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A deeply qualified, 28-month SAP Ariba displacement that has drifted into real jeopardy: the buyer set a decision window at the 21 Jan 2026 onsite that has passed, is now running the incumbent's beta program, and has been silent since 22 May 2026 while the close date was pushed for the third time to 31 Jul 2026. The fundamentals that once worked (confirmed pain, strong AI resonance, mapped executives) are all stale, and the proof points that would decide the deal (ANA POC, AI references, updated commercials) never landed.

win_position.text: Allstate is running a live SAP Ariba workshop/beta program that, per Gaurav on 21 May 2026, is consuming the technology resources needed for the Zycus POC, so the incumbent is getting hands-on evaluation time Zycus is not.
The buyer explicitly said it is not leaning either way (21 Jan 2026 onsite: 'Are you leaning one way or the other now? No'), and no buyer-voiced preference for Zycus has surfaced since.

The qualification base is real: the buyer called Zycus's RFP presentations a 'fabulous job' (21 Jan 2026), pain is confirmed across CLM, supplier risk and AP (Jun-Jul 2025 demos), and the economic buyer Casey McDowell and decision maker Mohit Bothra are mapped with Gaurav having offered to broker the Casey session.
Zycus's AI differentiation lands with this buyer: they demanded AI-in-production references, engaged hard on ANA and agentic intake at the onsite, and the AIS read is 8.0 Strong.

Gaurav is a gatekeeper rather than a champion: he runs the parallel SAP evaluation without pushback and has not replied since 22 May 2026, and no other advocate has emerged in 28 months of engagement.
The buyer's own decision timeline (decision 'in next 3/4 months' from 21 Jan 2026) expired around May with no selection, and the close date has been pushed three times since March 2026.

deal_momentum.text: No buyer response on any channel in 48 days: the last two-way exchange was Gaurav's 22 May 2026 email, and the rep follow-ups of 8 Jun and the 16 Jun and 29 Jun next-step chases drew nothing.
Close date pushed three times in four months (18 Mar, 20 May, 16 Jun 2026), a classic repeated-slip drag with the current date 22 days out and nothing scheduled.
No future meeting, POC date, or buyer-set milestone exists; the ANA POC offered on 25 Feb 2026 was never scheduled.

The slowdown is explained rather than ghosted: Gaurav told Zycus on 21-22 May 2026 that resources are tied up in the SAP Ariba beta and that he was taking the decision to leadership, which keeps the deal alive on the back burner rather than dead.
The evaluation is no longer in live process cadence: the January decision deadline passed in silence, so the quiet is a genuine stall, not RFP quiet.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer's last commitment was procedural, not advancing: 'Let me connect with our leadership and take their inputs on how to proceed' (Gaurav, 22 May 2026), and it has not been honored in 48 days.
Allstate never allocated the technology resources it acknowledged the Zycus POC needs (Gaurav, 21 May 2026), while finding resources for the SAP Ariba beta in the same period.

Commitment history was real when live: the buyer hosted the 21 Jan 2026 Chicago onsite with its product and sourcing leadership, co-scoped the reference plan, and agreed the SAP-vs-Zycus bake-off structure on 15 Dec 2025.

deal_risk.text: Incumbent capture risk: every week the SAP Ariba beta runs while Zycus waits, the switching case weakens, and the Ariba renewal clock (expiry ~12 months from Jan 2026) gives SAP a default path to win by inertia.

Single-thread risk at the worst moment: the decision has moved to leadership Zycus has no direct line to, through a gatekeeper who has gone quiet, with the EB Casey McDowell passive since the February exec-session attempts.
Forecast risk: Upside Key Deal with a 31 Jul 2026 close is not supportable on the evidence; the honest read is Pipeline with a Q4 2026 realistic close.

customer_preference.evidence: Asked directly at the 21 Jan 2026 onsite 'Are you leaning one way or the other now?', the buyer answered 'No... We are not.' No buyer-voiced preference has surfaced since; the only subsequent signal is the buyer investing in the SAP Ariba beta (Gaurav, 21 May 2026).

business_case.evidence: Pain is confirmed and specific (CLM redlining, invoice-to-PO/contract disconnects, supplier-risk fragmentation; Jun 2025 demos) but no dollar-quantified ROI or savings case has ever been voiced by Allstate, and no ROI workshop has occurred.

deal_movement.summary: Since mid-May the deal has moved only defensively: the buyer's 22 May 2026 reply routed the decision to leadership and then went silent, and the rep's response has been unanswered follow-ups and a third close-date push to 31 Jul 2026.

items.change: Next Step updated: Alexa to share the ANA SMS/WhatsApp innovation updates (built for McAfee) with Allstate to showcase innovation pace vs SAP and force a response on the beta outcome
Close date pushed from 2026-06-30 to 2026-07-31 (third push since March)
Rep follow-up to Gaurav on leadership direction; no reply (last activity on the record)
Last buyer reply: Gaurav to take the go-forward question to leadership ('Give me some time and I will get back to you')
Buyer disclosed the SAP Ariba workshop/beta program is consuming the technology resources needed for the Zycus POC

day_summary.overall: A quiet day on a deal that has been quiet for weeks. The last real touch was the deal owner's 8 Jun 2026 follow-up to Gaurav asking where the SAP Ariba beta and the evaluation stand, which remains unanswered; the buyer's last word was 22 May 2026, when Gaurav said he would take next steps to leadership. Since then the only movement has been the close date moving to 31 Jul 2026 on 16 Jun and a 29 Jun plan to send Allstate the new ANA innovation material to provoke a response.

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the single active threat and it is winning on access: Allstate is running a live internal SAP Ariba workshop/beta program (buyer-stated, 21 May 2026) that is consuming the resources needed for the Zycus POC, while the Ariba renewal (~12 months from Jan 2026) gives SAP a win-by-inertia path. Coupa and Ivalua remain in the Salesforce competitor field with no supporting evidence anywhere in calls, emails, or next steps and are dormant. SAP Fieldglass is a scoped VMS incumbent Zycus integrates with rather than displaces; Beeline and Icertis were single 2025 mentions, dormant.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: The buyer's decision lenses from the 21 Jan 2026 onsite are AI proven in production, realistic SAP lift-and-shift implementation, and innovation pace. Zycus is aligned on innovation substance but exposed on proof: the references and POC that would close the credibility gap never happened.

best_practice_check.summary: The deal's one live lever is credibility versus the incumbent at the leadership level. Every flag below serves that: prove AI in production, get above the gatekeeper, and stop letting the incumbent's beta be the only evaluation happening.
flags: Buyer doubt about Zycus AI at production scale is the active deal-killer: the AI references demanded on 21 Jan 2026 and the ANA POC offered 25 Feb 2026 are both undelivered while SAP runs its beta. Close this proof gap first, packaging the ANA innovation updates with named reference dates and the migration templates and readiness checklist promised in January.

Single-threaded through a silent gatekeeper: Gaurav has not replied in 48 days and the decision moved to leadership Zycus cannot see. Re-open the second thread via Casey McDowell (Executive Sponsor) and Mohit Bothra (Decision Maker), both already known to the deal.

No mutual plan against a real date: the close has slipped three times and the buyer's own decision window passed in May. When contact re-opens, co-author a dated plan (beta outcome, POC, references, commercials) against a realistic Q4 2026 close instead of chasing status.

champion_strength.summary: Gaurav is the closest thing to a champion but is functioning as a process gatekeeper: he coordinates logistics and relays status, has never been seen advocating for Zycus internally, runs the competing SAP Ariba beta without objection, and has been silent since 22 May 2026 with the decision now sitting with leadership above him.

scope_change.to: Full S2P suite; VMS explicitly kept out of scope (Fieldglass retained, per the 15 Jul 2025 VMS demo)
scope_change.detail: Scope has been stable since mid-2025: a full SAP Ariba lift-and-shift replacement with Merlin agentic AI (ANA, intake) as the differentiating layer, phased S2C then P2P per the implementation discussion of 21 Jan 2026.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus's AI story is the deal's strongest asset and is well positioned: the buyer engaged deeply on ANA and agentic intake at the 21 Jan 2026 onsite, demanded AI-specific references, and Zycus is answering with production evidence (Tata Play autonomous sourcing) plus the planned SMS/WhatsApp innovation updates (Next Step 2026-06-29). AIS 8.0 Strong.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Allstate is actively AI-forward: Mohit leads an AI and automation stream for procurement, the buying team probed agent accuracy, learning loops, and load handling in depth on 21 Jan 2026, and made AI-in-production references a decision condition.

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: Tasks/Events 120d + full Next_Step_History__c trail (Nov 2025 - Jun 2026) + field history
gaps: The 21 Jan 2026 onsite was read in full transcript; the Jun-Jul 2025 demo calls (demo#1-#3, VMS) were read from their Task Description Avoma summaries and prior-record extractions, not fresh full transcripts

drivers: ⚠️ The incumbent is out-evaluating us: Allstate is running a live SAP Ariba workshop/beta that, per Gaurav on 21 May 2026, is consuming the technology resources needed for the Zycus POC, and the Ariba renewal (~12 months from Jan 2026) gives SAP a win-by-inertia path. ► Force a beta-outcome date through Casey McDowell and land the ANA innovation proof now.

⚠️ No buyer-voiced preference exists: asked directly on 21 Jan 2026 whether they were leaning either way, the buyer said 'No... We are not', and nothing since has moved that needle. ► Deliver the two AI-adoption references and the POC to give the buyer a reason to prefer.

⚠️ Champion is weak and dark: Gaurav is a gatekeeper, not an advocate, silent 48 days since telling Zycus 'let me connect with our leadership' (22 May 2026), and no second thread is live. ► Re-open Mohit Bothra (Decision Maker) and the Casey exec session in parallel.

✅ The qualification base is genuinely strong: buyer-praised RFP performance ('fabulous job', 21 Jan 2026), confirmed pain across CLM, AP and supplier risk (Jun 2025 demos), EB and DM mapped, and an 8.0 Strong AI fit with the buyer demanding AI-specific proof.

✅ Differentiation is real and resonating: ANA and agentic intake live in production (Tata Play, presented 21 Jan 2026) versus SAP's still-beta AI story, with fresh SMS/WhatsApp agent updates queued (29 Jun 2026) as new evidence.

⚠️ The deal is far past its own clock: the buyer's Jan-2026 decision window ('next 3/4 months') expired unresolved, the stage has held ~6 months, and the close has slipped three times to 31 Jul 2026. ► Reset the forecast to Pipeline and re-baseline to a mutual Q4 plan.

⚠️ 48 days of buyer silence: the last two-way exchange was Gaurav's 22 May 2026 'give me some time', and the 8 Jun follow-up plus the 16 Jun and 29 Jun chases drew nothing. ► Lead the re-engagement with the new ANA innovation material and a dated ask, not another status check.
⚠️ Close date pushed three times in four months (18 Mar, 20 May, 16 Jun 2026), now 22 days out with nothing scheduled and no mutual plan. ► Co-author a realistic Q4 plan when contact re-opens.

⚠️ Not in live process cadence: the buyer's own decision deadline (three to four months from the 21 Jan onsite) passed in silence and the promised POC was never scheduled, so this quiet is a genuine stall, not RFP quiet. ► Get the beta-outcome date on paper.

⚠️ One-way outreach and full customer passivity: every touch since 22 May has been rep-initiated across email and next-step chases, with the buyer's evaluation energy visibly on the SAP Ariba beta. ► Escalate to the Casey McDowell exec line after three logged no-replies.

✅ The slowdown is explained, not ghosted: Gaurav named the cause (SAP beta resource contention, leadership consult) on 21-22 May 2026, which keeps the deal alive on the back burner rather than dead — decay, don't kill.
Focus now: one dated buyer commitment (beta outcome + POC date) within 14 days, secured above Gaurav if he stays silent.

prospect_requirements.item: Deliver the two AI-adoption customer reference calls (list and details shared in advance, prep calls done) that Allstate made a decision condition on 21 Jan 2026 and that were still pending on 28 Apr 2026.

zycus_commitments.item: Share the SAP lift-and-shift data-migration templates plus the ~20-item readiness checklist promised at the 21 Jan 2026 onsite ('would you be able to share few of those data templates... maybe a wrapper on top of it called checklist'); bundle with the reference scheduling.

Issue the updated commercial proposal that has been on the pending list since 13 Apr 2026 (Next Step: 'next reference, ANA POC, and updated commercials'), so refreshed pricing is on the table when leadership responds.

waiting_on_buyer.item: Leadership direction on how Allstate proceeds after the SAP Ariba beta — owed by Gaurav since 22 May 2026 ('Let me connect with our leadership... I will get back to you'); three follow-ups unanswered.
Technology-team resource allocation and dates for the ANA POC, blocked since 21 May 2026 by the SAP Ariba workshop/beta program; POC offered 25 Feb 2026 and never scheduled.

best_practices.item: Close the AI-credibility gap before the SAP beta concludes: send the ANA SMS/WhatsApp innovation proof (built for McAfee) to Gaurav and Mohit with a dated ask on the beta outcome — the buyer's doubt about Zycus AI at production scale is the active deal-killer.

Break the single thread: request a 30-minute executive session with Casey McDowell (Economic Buyer, previously open to a Chicago meeting) to hear leadership's post-beta direction, since the sole contact has been silent 48 days.

Re-baseline to a mutual close plan: sequence beta outcome, POC, references, and updated commercials against a realistic Q4 2026 close instead of a fourth one-month slip; flag the POC and references now for lead time.
